channel.title: 2. Setup :: Hands-on Amazon GameLift: Deploy Your First Multiplayer Game
channel.description: Duration: ~20 minutes Two paths — pick the one that matches how you’re attending: At an AWS event → a temporary account and a browser IDE are provided (2.1) In your own account → install a few tools locally (2.2) Then everyone clones the repo, bootstraps CDK, and deploys the game backend.

item.title: Path A: At an AWS Event
item.description: Info Follow this page only if you’re at an AWS-hosted event with a Workshop Studio access code. Otherwise skip to Path B: Own Account. 1. Join the event Open Workshop Studio and enter the access code provided by your instructor. Accept the terms and click Join event. You now have a temporary AWS account — nothing you do today costs you anything. 2. Open your development environment The event account comes pre-provisioned with a cloud development machine (VS Code in the browser) that already has every tool installed:

item.title: Path B: Own Account
item.description: Warning Cost estimate: the resources deployed today cost roughly $0.20/hour while running (one c5.large GameLift instance + serverless components). The Cleanup module removes everything; a full 2-hour run typically costs under $1. 1. AWS account & credentials You need an account with administrator-level access (CDK creates IAM roles, GameLift fleets, CloudFront distributions). Configure credentials locally:

item.title: Clone & Bootstrap
item.description: 1. Clone the repository (AWS event path: already cloned at ~/gamelift-workshop — just cd into it.) git clone https://github.com/malphi/gamelift-workshop.git cd gamelift-workshop 2. Install dependencies (cd infra && npm install) (cd backend && npm install) (cd frontend && npm install) 3. Bootstrap CDK CDK needs a one-time bootstrap per account/region (creates an S3 bucket and roles it deploys through):

item.title: Deploy the Game Backend
item.description: What you’re deploying Two CDK stacks — the web half of the game, no GameLift yet: PixelRushBackendStack — API Gateway + Lambda + DynamoDB (login, garage, shop, leaderboard, matchmaking API) and a WebSocket API for notifications PixelRushFrontendStack — the Phaser web client on CloudFront + private S3 1. Deploy From the infra/ directory:
